title: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria
taxonomy: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria, also known as the walking fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger group known as the Thelypteridaceae or the wood ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is often included in the broader group of Thelypteris, but some taxonomists recognize Cyclosorus as a distinct genus within the family Thelypteridaceae. The classification of Cyclosorus chamaeotaria can be complex due to ongoing debates in the field of fern taxonomy. As such, the classification of this species may vary depending on the source.
uses: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria, also known as the Chamaeotrys fern, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health conditions. In some cultures, the fern is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, it is also used to treat digestive problems and as an antiseptic. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cyclosorus chamaeotaria for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria, also known as the creeping fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. constituents: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria is a plant species that contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides. These compounds have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in this plant have been identified as kaempferol and quercetin, which are known for their potential health benefits. Further research is necessary to fully understand the active constituents and their effects on human health.
parts: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria is a species of fern, and its medicinal parts are primarily the rhizomes and roots. These parts are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. preparations: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria is a species of fern that has been utilized in traditional herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. side_effects: Cyclosorus chamaeotaria, also known as the walking fern, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia.
